publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.unittitle: Christian Church of Trumbulls Corners records

origination.label: creator
origination.corpname: Christian Church of Trumbull\'s Corners (Newfield, N.Y.)

did.abstract: One volume (1854-1920) of church records which contain minutes of Quarterly Fellowship and Annual Meetings; accounts of church services (including marriages and funerals), and socials; and annual reports to the conference which include membership and financial information. Of note is the number of women who preached to the congregation.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: ORGANIZATIONAL HISTORY
bioghist.p: The Christian Church at Trumbull's Corners was organized on May 20, 1854 in school district No. 12. The first pastor was Reverend Ezra Chase. In 1858 the church began to erect an edifice, which was dedicated on December 23, 1858. The church was affiliated with the Tioga River Conference until 1906, when it became part of the New York Central Christian Conference. The church incorporated itself as the Christian Church of Newfield on June 24, 1858 and as the First Christian Church of Newfield on February 20, 1908.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: The collection contains one volume (1854-1920) of church records which contain minutes of Quarterly Fellowship and Annual meetings, 1854, 57-61, 1886-1920; accounts of church services (including marriages and funerals) and socials; and annual reports to the conference which include membership and financial information. Minutes record new members received, letters of dismission granted, the appointment of individuals to attend the Conference meetings, the election of officers, and attendance at meetings.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Christian Church of Trumbull's Corners records, #6057. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

controlaccess.geogname: Tompkins County (N.Y.) -- Religious life and customs.
controlaccess.subject: Church records and registers -- New York (State) -- Newfield.
Christian sects -- New York (State) -- Newfield.
controlaccess.corpname: New York Central Christian Conference (Newfield, N.Y.)
First Christian Church of Newfield (Newfield, N.Y.)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Listing the location of deeds of church property Articles of Faith

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: May 20, 1854 Account of formation of the church and minutes copied from the old church record commence.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Minutes 1854, 1857, 1858 December 23, 1858 meeting house dedicated

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1858-1861 many new members

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: June 24, 1858 church incorporates

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: July 18, 1859 resolutions on church organization passed l86l minutes end

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Record of membership which includes date of entrance, when dies, withdrew married, or letter granted. 1854-1916

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: warrent deed to church property made August 3, 1859

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: records continue where the old church left off, 3/23/86

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: October 14, 1888 collection for foreign missions

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: June 13, 1891 a "stirring temperance lecture was given by G. F. Jones of Syracuse, and a collection taken up." There was some discussion of organizing a Y of G.T., but there was not sufficient action on the part of people of the community.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: June 28, 1891 a rousing sermon on Baptism yields 7 new members

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: November 28, 1891 report of a committee for determining the necessity and feasibility of repairing the church 1892 first mention of a sabbath school

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: January 17, 1892 Resolution to condemn someone who placed a toy on the tree at Christmas which offended a brother and his family

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: March 26, 1892 vote on bids for church repairs. Services were held in the M.E. church during the repairs.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: April 30, 1892 membership reported. April 30, 1892 discussion whether or not to copy the old church record

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: September 29, 1892 Rev. J.M. Scott of the Unitarian Church in Ithaca and the Rev. Mr. Jones of the Baptist Church in Ithaca, lecture on temperance.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: December 5, 1982 an 850 pound bell is placed in the steeple and chimesfor the first time.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: December 31, 1892 reported that efforts to get a minister to come and aid them in a protracted effort in the near future failed.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: June 8, 1893 The Tioga River Christina Conference, held at the church, is "especially interesting as a number of young ministers and two lady ministers were among the list of speakers."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: woman preaches to the congregation December 2,3 1894 the congregation hopes for a revival, which continues until January 3 and 4

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: January 25, 1895 a lecture on temperance

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: July 19, September 13, September 20, October 4 a woman preaches

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: the pastor's wife preaches in his absence

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: nNovember 13, 1898 temperance lecture

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: May 11, 1899 a temperance lecturer, a woman, sent by the Grand Lodge of G of T.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: June 8/11 The 55th annual session of the Tioga River Christian Conference, meeting at the church, resolves to protest against the seating of Brigham H. Roberts, a Mormon, as rep. of Utah to the U.S. Congress.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 12/30/99 the church begins to call themselves the First Christian Church of Newfield at Trumbull's Comers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: February 8, 1903 discussion of a revival

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: December 26, 1903 plans for celebrating the church's 50th anniversary are discussed.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: December 1905 many new members.
